name: Vallabhacharya
dates_notes: Mughal-era court records and sectarian chronicles align.

dates_evidence.description: Mughal-period court records of his life and patronage.

core_thesis:
Vallabha's *śuddhādvaita* — pure non-dualism — turns on a single technical claim: *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda*, transformation without modification. Brahman, identified with Kṛṣṇa, is the sole reality and is absolutely non-dual; the world and the *jīvas* are equally real, precisely as Brahman's own *aṃśas*; and the relation between Brahman and world is one of genuine *pariṇāma* in which Brahman is nevertheless *avikṛta* — un-modified, undiminished, not changed in itself by what proceeds from it.

The *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* on [*Brahma-Sūtra* 1.4.26](cite://badarayana/brahma-sutra/1.4.26) (*ātmakṛteḥ pariṇāmāt*) is the load-bearing text. Vallabha reads the sūtra as licensing *pariṇāma* directly: the world is the real transformation of Brahman by self-action (*ātmakṛti*). The qualifier *avikṛta* does the structural work that distinguishes the position from its rivals. It is not Sāṃkhya *pariṇāma*, in which *prakṛti* is genuinely modified; *prakṛti*-style modification would compromise Brahman's *paripūrṇa*, undiminishable nature. It is not Advaita *vivarta*, in which the world is a non-real appearance of an unmodified substrate; the world is *satya*, not *mithyā*. It is *avikṛta-pariṇāma*: Brahman really transforms, and Brahman is really not modified, and these are co-tenable because the transformation is of an order in which *sat-cit-ānanda* are unfolded (*āvirbhāva*) and refolded (*tirobhāva*) as conditions on Brahman's own qualities, not as alterations of Brahman itself.

The ontology of *jīva* and *jagat* falls out of this directly. *Jīva* is *aṃśa* of Brahman manifesting *sat* and *cit* with *ānanda* concealed (*tirohita*). *Jagat* is *aṃśa* manifesting *sat* with *cit* and *ānanda* concealed. Liberation is not the realisation of an acosmic identity but the *āvirbhāva* of *ānanda* in the *jīva* — concealed quality unfolded — through Bhagavān's *anugraha*.

Soteriologically the school's distinctive path is *Puṣṭi-Mārga*, the path of grace. Liberation is bestowed by Kṛṣṇa's free *anugraha* on the *bhakta* he selects. *Seva* — Deity-service — is the operative practice. The position should not be conflated with Śaṅkara's Advaita despite the shared *advaita* label; *śuddhādvaita* and Advaita differ exactly on the world's status, and the difference is structural, not verbal.

engaged_works.title: Anu-Bhashya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored for the portion Vallabha himself completed; the third-pāda-onward continuation was by his son Viṭṭhalanātha.
engaged_works.summary: The *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* is Vallabha's Brahma-Sūtra commentary and the school-defining bhāṣya of *śuddhādvaita*. The name *aṇu* ("small") refers both to the terse glossing style and to the work's self-presentation alongside the lost or projected *Bṛhad-Bhāṣya*. Vallabha completed roughly the first two and a half *adhyāyas* before his death; the remainder was supplied by his son Viṭṭhalanātha. The construal of the *sūtras* throughout is consistent. Brahman is the substantial cause of the world by genuine but unmodifying transformation — *avikṛta-pariṇāma* — and the load-bearing *adhikaraṇa* is 1.4.26, *ātmakṛteḥ pariṇāmāt*, where the *sūtra*'s *pariṇāma* term is taken at face value. The *jīva* is *aṃśa* with *ānanda* concealed; *jagat* is *aṃśa* with *cit* and *ānanda* concealed; *māyā-vāda* is rejected. Polemically the work targets both Śaṅkara, for treating the world as *mithyā* and Brahman as inactive, and Rāmānuja, for the *śarīra-śarīrin* qualification that Vallabha takes as superfluous once *avikṛta-pariṇāma* is admitted: if Brahman can transform without being modified, no further qualifying body-relation is needed to insulate Brahman from suffering. The bhāṣya is the canonical Puṣṭimārga doctrinal reference and the primary source for *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda* in Vallabha's own voice.

engaged_works.title: Tattvartha-Dipa-Nibandha
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored systematic exposition of Śuddhādvaita.
engaged_works.summary: The *Tattvārtha-Dīpa-Nibandha* is Vallabha's three-part systematic exposition of *śuddhādvaita*. Three *prakaraṇa*s organise the work. *Śāstrārtha-Prakaraṇa* (book 1) sets out the *prasthāna-traya* method and Vallabha's interpretive principles, including his hermeneutic for handling apparently conflicting Upaniṣadic statements ([*Tattvārtha-Dīpa-Nibandha* 1](cite://vallabha/tattvartha-dipa-nibandha/1), *kāriks* 1–104). *Sarva-Nirṇaya-Prakaraṇa* (book 2) fixes the school's settled doctrinal positions across Brahman, *jīva*, *jagat*, *bandha*, *mokṣa*, and *bhakti* ([*Tattvārtha-Dīpa-Nibandha* 2](cite://vallabha/tattvartha-dipa-nibandha/2), the *prameya-nirṇaya* sequence). *Bhāgavata-Artha-Prakaraṇa* (book 3) establishes the *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* as *pramāṇa-śiromaṇi* and reads its central themes through *śuddhādvaita* ([*Tattvārtha-Dīpa-Nibandha* 3](cite://vallabha/tattvartha-dipa-nibandha/3), anchored on *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 1.1.1 and 1.3.28). The *Nibandha* is the school's most accessible synoptic statement; it is free from the *sūtra*-form constraints of the *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* and from the verse-by-verse pacing of the *Subodhinī*, and so permits Vallabha to set out *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda* as the school's signature ontological thesis and *Puṣṭi-Mārga* as its operative soteriology in directly argumentative form. The polemical framing against Advaita and Viśiṣṭādvaita that all later Puṣṭimārga scholasticism inherits is established here.

engaged_works.title: Subodhini on Bhagavata
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored Bhāgavata commentary; Vallabha completed a portion, with Viṭṭhalanātha continuing.
engaged_works.summary: The *Subodhinī* is Vallabha's verse-by-verse commentary on the *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* and is the most extensive of his works. The *Bhāgavata* is read as the *naijagama-bhāṣya* — the natural commentary on the *Brahma-Sūtras* and the Upaniṣads — and therefore as the supreme *pramāṇa* for *śuddhādvaita* (*Subodhinī* prefatory *upodghāta* on *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 1.1.1). Vallabha completed substantial portions before his death, notably on books one through three and most of the tenth book; Viṭṭhalanātha continued the work. The commentary is doctrinally precise. Brahman is Kṛṣṇa with all *guṇas* manifest (*Subodhinī* on *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 1.3.28, *kṛṣṇas tu bhagavān svayam*). *Jīva* and *jagat* are *aṃśa* through *āvirbhāva–tirobhāva* — manifestation and concealment of qualities — without *māyā* (the *āvirbhāva–tirobhāva* analysis at *Subodhinī* on *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 2.5–6, the cosmogony cantos). *Māyā-vāda* is rejected at the verses Advaitins standardly recruit (e.g. *Subodhinī* on *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 2.9.32–33, *ṛte 'rthaṃ yat pratīyeta*, against the Advaitic *anirvacanīya*-reading). It is also a major work of devotional exegesis, especially on the tenth book, where Vraja-*līlā* is read as the central self-disclosure of Bhagavān and the model for *Puṣṭi-Mārga* *seva* (*Subodhinī* on *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* 10.21 ff., the *veṇu-gīta* and the autumnal *līlā* sequence). The *Subodhinī* is the principal Puṣṭimārga reference for *Bhāgavata*-interpretation and the spiritual heart of the lineage's textual practice.

engaged_works.title: Shodasha-Granthah
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sixteen short treatises foundational for the Puṣṭimārga.
engaged_works.summary: The *Ṣoḍaśa-Granthāḥ* are sixteen short Sanskrit tracts that together codify *Puṣṭi-Mārga* devotional practice and self-understanding. Among the most consequential are the *Yamunāṣṭakam* (eight verses on the river Yamunā as Kṛṣṇa-presence), the *Bāla-Bodha* (an introductory doctrinal primer), the *Siddhānta-Muktāvalī* (a verse summary of *śuddhādvaita*), the *Puṣṭi-Pravāha-Maryādā* (on the typology of *jīvas* and the operation of *anugraha*), the *Siddhānta-Rahasya* (the doctrinal foundation of the *brahma-saṃbandha* initiation that joins the *bhakta* to Kṛṣṇa), the *Navaratnam* ("nine jewels," a consolatory teaching on dependence on Kṛṣṇa), and the *Antaḥkaraṇa-Prabodha* and *Sarva-Nirṇaya* tracts on inner discernment. As a corpus the *Ṣoḍaśa-Granthāḥ* function as the lineage's portable devotional and doctrinal manual: short enough to be memorised, doctrinally precise, and addressed directly to the practitioner of *Puṣṭi-Mārga seva*. They are recited and studied in Puṣṭimārga households to this day.

key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
tat brahmaiva samavāyi kāraṇam.
kutaḥ? samanvayāt samyag-anugatatvāt.
asti bhāti priyatvena sac-cid-ānanda-rūpeṇānvayāt.
nāma-rūpayoḥ kārya-dharmatvāt.

pada_analysis.gloss: from coherent pervasion; from systematic connection

pada_analysis.morphology: instr sg neut; first member of sandhi-bundle sac-cid-ānanda-rūpeṇānvayāt
pada_analysis.gloss: in the form of being, consciousness, and bliss

pada_analysis.morphology: abl sg masc; second member of sandhi-bundle rūpeṇānvayāt = rūpeṇa + anvayāt

pada_analysis.gloss: because of having the status of effect-properties

samasa_vigrahas.resolution: sat ca cit cānandaś ca rūpaṃ yasya tat
samasa_vigrahas.note: Dvandva base embedded in a possessive/tatpurusha-style formation.
The condition of being a property belonging to an effect.

karaka_structure.note: Subject of the predicative claim and of the finite verbs asti and bhāti.
Mode/instrumental expression of how the pervasion is encountered.
Instrumental of form: Brahman's pervasion as sat, cit, and ānanda.
Genitive relation to kārya-dharmatva: name and form belong on the effect side.

verb_modality.note: Finite copular verb in present indicative.
Present indicative: Brahman shines forth as the pervading reality.

key_passages.english_close: That Brahman itself is the inhering cause. Why? Because of coherent pervasion, from its proper pervasion as being, shining, and dear, in the form of sat-cit-ānanda; and because name and form have the status of properties belonging to the effect.
key_passages.english_paraphrase: Vallabha argues that Brahman is the world's real material cause because the whole world is pervaded by Brahman's sat-cit-ānanda, while differentiated names and forms belong only to the level of effected manifestation.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: inhering/material cause; that cause whose own being persists in the effect

key_passages.why_this_passage: This is a fair doctrinal center of Vallabha's ontology because it states, in compressed form, why Brahman is not merely an external maker but the world's real constitutive cause. It also shows the hallmark Śuddhādvaita move: names and forms are derivative effect-properties, while the underlying reality remains Brahman's own sat-cit-ānanda.
key_passages.source_edition: Anubhasyam. Calcutta: Asiatic Society, Bibliotheca Indica, 1897; transcribed from materials/primary_texts/sanskrit/vedanta/full_corpus/vallabha_anubhasya_1897_archive.pdf, checked against the same scan's Internet Archive OCR sidecars (anubhasyam00vall).

key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
atra brahmajñānenāvidyā-nivṛttyā puruṣottama-prāptau svarūpa-yogyatā sampādyate.
tato jīve svīyatvena varaṇe bhakti-bhāvāt sahakāri-yogyatā-sampādya puruṣottama-prāptir bhavatīti nirṇeyate.
bhaktyāham ekayā grāhyaḥ.

pada_analysis.morphology: instr sg neut; first member of sandhi-bundle brahmajñānenāvidyā-nivṛttyā
instr sg fem; second member of sandhi-bundle brahmajñānenāvidyā-nivṛttyā

pada_analysis.gloss: fitness of one's own true condition
in being chosen; in divine choosing
having brought about the auxiliary fitness

pada_analysis.morphology: 3sg pres ind P; first member of sandhi-bundle bhavatīti = bhavati + iti
indecl; quotation-particle, here second member of bhavatīti

samasa_vigrahas.note: Fitness for one's own proper state.
Gerund built on a causative accomplishment of an assisting qualification.

karaka_structure.note: Instrument by which initial qualification is achieved.
Co-instrument: removal of ignorance.
Domain with respect to which the qualification is relevant.
What is accomplished in the first sentence.
Location: in the soul.
Condition/context of divine choosing.
From the devotional state the assisting qualification follows.
Resulting attainment named as the conclusion.
Exclusive instrument in the final citation.
Subject of the predicative gerundive grāhyaḥ.

verb_modality.note: Present passive: the qualification is accomplished.
Present indicative: attainment comes to be.
Present passive: this doctrinal conclusion is determined.

key_passages.english_close: Here, by Brahman-knowledge and by the cessation of ignorance, fitness for one's own true state is accomplished with respect to the attainment of Puruṣottama. Then, when the soul is chosen as His own, and with devotion as its disposition, having brought about the assisting fitness, attainment of Puruṣottama comes to be: thus it is determined. 'I am to be grasped by devotion alone.'
key_passages.english_paraphrase: For Vallabha, knowledge removes ignorance and prepares the soul, but actual attainment of Puruṣottama is completed in and through bhakti under divine choosing.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: the supreme Person; Vallabha's fully personal Brahman identified with Kṛṣṇa
devotional participation and attachment to Bhagavān, not mere sentiment
fitness for one's own proper spiritual condition

key_passages.why_this_passage: This is a fair selection because it does not isolate a merely devotional flourish; it shows the actual Śuddhādvaita sequence by which brahmajñāna removes ignorance, yet bhakti and divine choosing complete Puruṣottama-attainment. It also comes from the Vallabha-authored early portion of the bhāṣya, so it represents his own soteriological architecture rather than only later Puṣṭimārga elaboration.
key_passages.source_edition: Anubhasyam. Calcutta: Asiatic Society, Bibliotheca Indica, 1897; transcribed from materials/primary_texts/sanskrit/vedanta/full_corpus/vallabha_anubhasya_1897_archive.pdf, checked against the same scan's Internet Archive OCR sidecars (anubhasyam00vall).

key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
tad ātmānaṃ svayam akuruta iti svasyaiva kartṛ-bhāvāt avikṛtatva-śravaṇāc cālaukikatvam.
tathāpi pariṇamate kāryākāreṇa.
avikṛtam eva pariṇamate suvarṇaṃ sarvāṇi ca tejasāni.

pada_analysis.gloss: because of the condition of being agent
because of the scriptural hearing of unmodifiedness
into the form of the effect

samasa_vigrahas.note: State or condition of being the agent.
Scriptural hearing/statement that the cause remains unmodified.
The form or configuration of the effect.

karaka_structure.note: Object of the quoted verb akuruta.
Subject of akuruta and of the subsequent pariṇamate claim.
Genitive qualifying the agentive condition: its own agency.
Instrumental of form: transformation as effect-form.
Range of products exemplified by the gold analogy.

verb_modality.note: Imperfect middle in the Upaniṣadic citation 'it made itself'.
Present middle: the cause transforms into effect-form.

key_passages.english_close: 'It made itself as itself': because there is the state of its own agency, and because scripture speaks of its being unmodified, this is non-ordinary. Even so, it transforms into the form of the effect. Gold, while itself unmodified, indeed transforms into all golden products.
key_passages.english_paraphrase: Vallabha's point is that Brahman's causality is a real manifestation, but not a loss of intrinsic nature: the gold remains gold while appearing as many ornaments.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: real manifestation or transformation without intrinsic alteration

key_passages.why_this_passage: This is one of the most representative places to see why Vallabha cannot be collapsed into Advaita's vivarta theory: he explicitly insists on real pariṇāma while preserving Brahman's avikṛtatva. The gold analogy is not an ornamental example here; it is the doctrinal mechanism by which Śuddhādvaita makes the world genuinely real without making Brahman compromised or diminished.
key_passages.source_edition: Anubhasyam. Calcutta: Asiatic Society, Bibliotheca Indica, 1897; transcribed from materials/primary_texts/sanskrit/vedanta/full_corpus/vallabha_anubhasya_1897_archive.pdf, checked against the same scan's Internet Archive OCR sidecars (anubhasyam00vall).

key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
jīvo nāma brahmaṇo 'ṃśaḥ.
kutaḥ? nānāvyapadeśāt.
na hi brahma niraṃśaṃ sāṃśam iti vā kvacid loke siddhaṃ, vedaika-samadhigamyatvāt.
mamaivāṃśo jīvaloke jīvabhūtaḥ sanātana iti smaryate.

pada_analysis.gloss: because it is knowable only through the Veda
in the world of living beings

samasa_vigrahas.note: The condition of being ascertainable only through Veda.
The realm/world of embodied living beings.

karaka_structure.note: Genitive relation: the jīva is a part of Brahman.
Subject of the initial predicative sentence.
Location of ordinary, non-scriptural ascertainment.
Quoted smṛti predication about the eternal jīva.
Locative framing for the Gītā citation.

verb_modality.note: Present passive: the point is supported by smṛti.

key_passages.english_close: The jīva indeed is a part of Brahman. Why? Because of manifold designation. For Brahman is not established anywhere in ordinary experience as either partless or as having parts; it is knowable only through the Veda. 'An eternal part of Me in the world of living beings, having become a jīva' is remembered in smṛti.
key_passages.english_paraphrase: Vallabha does not treat the jīva's relation to Brahman as a mere metaphor: the self is a real aṃśa, and that relation is fixed by scriptural teaching rather than by prior worldly categories.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: real part or portion; not a merely figurative emanation

key_passages.why_this_passage: This is the cleanest short statement of Vallabha's jīva-Brahman relation in the accessible authored corpus: the soul is a real aṃśa, and the doctrine is anchored in scriptural designation rather than reduced to an empirical category. That makes it a fair representative passage for Śuddhādvaita's refusal both of absolute identity without remainder and of total separateness.
key_passages.source_edition: Anubhasyam. Calcutta: Asiatic Society, Bibliotheca Indica, 1897; transcribed from materials/primary_texts/sanskrit/vedanta/full_corpus/vallabha_anubhasya_1897_archive.pdf, checked against the same scan's Internet Archive OCR sidecars (anubhasyam00vall).
